evidence: Live defect reproduced first via direct pi-context read-block-page calls on all 18 blocks: research and session-notes threw MigrationRegistry errors, all 16 others read clean. Root cause traced to code: updateContext merge branch, resolveConflict, and write-schema --operation replace never registered a migration declaration, unlike resyncSchema catalog-ahead migrate branch. Fix (registerCatalogMigrationChainIfKnown) wired into all three sites; 8 new tests cover registration + refuse-to-fabricate for each; full pi-context suite 1305 pass / 0 fail, full monorepo build/check/test exit 0. Data repair applied directly via the sanctioned npx tsx -e path (no dedicated CLI op exists for this internal helper); both research and session-notes confirmed reading clean via the real CLI, independently, before and after merge to main. Fresh adversarial re-audit (different agent) found zero defects across seven probes including the implementer own deviation (write-schema wiring moved from schema-write.ts to the ops-registry.ts op handler to avoid double-firing against writeSchemaCheckedForDir shared callers). docs-surface-sync audit found and corrected three stale op-description/README passages that scoped migration registration to the catalog-ahead resync arm only.

criteria_results.criterion: registerCatalogMigrationChainIfKnown exists adjacent to resyncSchema, reuses findCatalogMigrationChain, never fabricates an identity decl when no catalog chain resolves

updateContext merge branch, resolveConflict, and write-schema --operation replace each call the new helper after their existing stampBaselineFromBody call and thread the result into their report shape
criteria_results.evidence: write-schema site wired at the ops-registry.ts op handler rather than schema-write.ts per the plan, since writeSchemaCheckedForDir is shared by all three callers and in-place registration there would double-fire; verified by adversarial audit as the correct fix, not a coverage gap

criteria_results.criterion: schema-write.ts doc comment updated for the resolved nuance
new tests cover all three call sites registering a known chain and all three refusing to fabricate when no chain is known; existing tests for all three unchanged
criteria_results.evidence: 8 new tests, all passing, independently re-run by the adversarial auditor

criteria_results.criterion: research and session-notes blocks both read successfully via the real pi-context CLI read path after the fix and data repair
all 18 blocks re-read successfully via the real read path
build, check, and full test suite exit 0
CHANGELOG entry naming this does not close FGAP-140
criteria_results.evidence: worded without a raw substrate-ID citation per the citation-rot AST-scanner gate
